name: Kaneohe Sandbar (Ahu o Laka)
tagline: A sandbar in the middle of Kaneohe Bay where you stand in knee-deep turquoise water with the Koolaus as backdrop.
parking: Park at Heeia Kea Boat Harbor; various tour operators depart from here
hours: Tour operators run morning and afternoon trips; sandbar accessible during low tide
bestTime: Low tide for maximum sandbar exposure; morning trips for calmer water and better light
kamaaina: Some tour operators offer kamaaina rates — ask when booking
talkStory: Known to Hawaiians as Ahu o Laka, this sandbar is considered sacred -- traditionally an offering site for hula practitioners honoring Laka, goddess of hula and forest plants. At low tide, the bar rises to create a shallow turquoise lagoon perfect for toddlers. Insider tip: rent a kayak from Heeia Kea Harbor instead of booking a tour boat -- it is a short paddle, far cheaper, and you get the sandbar to yourself early morning.
